questions.question: What problem do attention variants solve that Flash Attention doesn't?
options: Numerical instability in softmax

Flash Attention hides the O(N²) activation MEMORY but every token still attends to every token — the arithmetic stays O(N²); variants change the TOPOLOGY of the attention matrix itself (128K-context Llama 3 70B = 16 billion entries per layer)
Tokenizer vocabulary size
Gradient vanishing in deep stacks
questions.explanation: Three families: sliding window (attend to W neighbors), sparse/block (pre-chosen pairs only), and differential (subtract two attention maps).

questions.question: How does sliding window attention change the cost, and what does it do to the KV cache?
options: It doubles cost for better quality
Each token attends only to the last W positions, dropping compute to O(N·W) — and only the last W tokens of K/V need caching: a Gemma-3-ish 1024-window config at 128K context cuts the KV cache 128x
It only changes training, not inference
It removes the need for positions
questions.explanation: The cache reduction is often the bigger win than the FLOPs: serving memory is the scarce resource at long context.

questions.question: How do SWA models avoid losing long-range retrieval?
options: They can't — SWA forbids long range

Interleave SWA layers with full-attention layers (Gemma 3's 5:1 ratio is the textbook default) — and stacked causal SWA grows the receptive field anyway: after L layers, information flows L×W tokens back through overlapping windows
By doubling the window every layer
By adding more attention heads
questions.explanation: Mistral 7B's stack relied on exactly that forward flow. The occasional global layer is the insurance policy for direct long-range lookup.

questions.question: What are the canonical sparse-attention patterns?
options: Random dropout of attention scores

Local + strided (last W tokens plus every stride-th earlier, O(N·√N) — OpenAI sparse transformer), local + global tokens + random links (Longformer/BigBird), and learned block sparsity skipped at kernel level (DeepSeek's Native Sparse Attention, 2025)
Diagonal-only attention
Alphabetically grouped tokens
questions.explanation: The progression runs from hand-designed patterns to LEARNED ones — NSA decides which (Q,K) blocks matter and stays FlashAttention-compatible.

questions.question: What does differential attention fix?
options: Slow softmax kernels

The "attention sink" — weight bleeding into the first few tokens regardless of relevance; computing two attention maps with separate Q/K projections and SUBTRACTING one from the other cancels the common-mode noise (Microsoft DIFF Transformer, 2024)
Overlong context windows
Tokenization artifacts
questions.explanation: Like a differential amplifier in electronics: shared noise cancels in the subtraction. Frontier models mix all three variants — most layers SWA, some global, a few differential heads cleaning up retrieval.
